places.name: Shin-Yokohama Ramen Museum
places.description: This historical theme park celebrates all things noodle.

places.name: Hara Model Railway Museum
places.description: The largest collection of model trains in the world is actually run using full-size locomotive technology.

places.name: Tin Toy Museum Kitahara Collection
places.description: More tin toys than you can shake a clapping monkey at.

places.name: Umi-Shibaura Station
places.description: Passengers are not allowed to set foot outside this station, but you can still enjoy spectacular views of the sea from the platform.

places.name: Taya Caves
places.description: A network of ancient tunnels and vaulted chambers with mystical Buddhist reliefs, built for ascetic training.

places.name: Green Thumb
places.description: Home to herbal cosmetics, teas, and a unique set of dolls known as kitchen witches.

places.name: Kokudō Station
places.description: Unchanged for decades, World War II-era bullet holes and nostalgic ghost signs still haunt this desolate train station.

places.name: Pink Holiday
places.description: Barbies stare from the walls, sit on the tables, and even pop out of cakes at this quirky cafe.
